rawIndexableTextContent: Represents a vault in an encrypted cloud file system.  Overview A vault is a password-derived directory path that isolates encrypted data. Devices with different passwords will create different vaults, each invisible to the other. The vault path derivation is compatible with the ObjC Ensembles 2 CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystemVaultInfo.
title: VaultInfo
summary: Represents a vault in an encrypted cloud file system.

rawIndexableTextContent: Unified encryption/decryption interface with automatic format detection on decrypt.
title: FileEncryptor
summary: Unified encryption/decryption interface with automatic format detection on decrypt.

rawIndexableTextContent: Encrypts data using the specified format.  The plaintext data to encrypt. The password to derive the encryption key from. The encryption format to use (default: .modern). Return Value The encrypted data.
title: encrypt(data:password:format:)
summary: Encrypts data using the specified format.

rawIndexableTextContent: The password used for encryption and vault path derivation.
title: password
summary: The password used for encryption and vault path derivation.

rawIndexableTextContent: Decrypts data, auto-detecting the format from the version byte.  The encrypted data. The password used to encrypt the data. Return Value The decrypted plaintext data.
title: decrypt(data:password:)
summary: Decrypts data, auto-detecting the format from the version byte.

rawIndexableTextContent: The underlying cloud file system.
title: cloudFileSystem

title: passwordDependentPath

rawIndexableTextContent: An encryption wrapper for any Ensembles cloud backend. Overview EnsemblesEncrypted provides EncryptedCloudFileSystem, a wrapper that encrypts files before uploading and decrypts after downloading. It can wrap any CloudFileSystem implementation — CloudKit, Google Drive, WebDAV, or your own custom backend — to provide end-to-end encryption for sync data. Encrypted files are stored in a password-derived vault directory with a .cdecrypt extension. This is backward compatible with the Ensembles 2 Objective-C CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystem. Encryption Formats Two encryption formats are available: .modern (default) — AES-256-GCM. Fast, authenticated encryption using Apple’s CryptoKit. Recommended for new apps. .legacy — RNCryptor v3 format. Use this for backward compatibility with Ensembles 2 encrypted data. The backend auto-detects the encryption format when downloading, so a .modern client can read .legacy files and vice versa. The format setting only controls how new files are encrypted. Setup Wrap any cloud file system with encryption:  For more control over the vault, use the VaultInfo initializer:  When to Use Encryption Ensembles sync data is stored as opaque binary files, so even without encryption the raw data is not human-readable. However, it could be deserialized by someone with access to your cloud storage. For CloudKit, Apple encrypts CKAsset data at rest. With Advanced Data Protection (ADP) enabled, assets are end-to-end encrypted. Wrapping CloudKit with EncryptedCloudFileSystem adds a layer of protection that doesn’t depend on the user enabling ADP. For third-party backends (Google Drive, OneDrive, WebDAV, etc.), the storage provider can see the raw files. Wrapping with EncryptedCloudFileSystem ensures data is encrypted before it leaves the device, regardless of what the provider does. How It Works Vault directory — All encrypted files are stored inside a vault directory in the cloud, derived from the password. Different passwords create different vaults, so the same cloud account can host multiple independent encrypted sync stores. Upload — When Ensembles uploads a file, the wrapper encrypts it to a local temporary file, uploads the encrypted version with a .cdecrypt extension, then removes the temporary file. Download — When Ensembles downloads a file, the wrapper downloads the encrypted .cdecrypt file, auto-detects the format (modern or legacy), decrypts it to a local temporary file, and returns the decrypted content. Backward compatibility — The .legacy format uses RNCryptor v3, the same encryption format used by Ensembles 2’s CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystem. Apps migrating from E2 to E3 can read existing encrypted cloud data without re-encrypting. Wrapping Other Backends The wrapper works with any CloudFileSystem:  Dependencies This module has no external dependencies beyond Foundation, CryptoKit, and the core Ensembles framework.
title: EnsemblesEncrypted
summary: An encryption wrapper for any Ensembles cloud backend.

rawIndexableTextContent: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.  A value to compare. Another value to compare. Discussion Inequality is the inverse of equality. For any values a and b, a != b implies that a == b is false. This is the default implementation of the not-equal-to operator (!=) for any type that conforms to Equatable.
summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.

rawIndexableTextContent: AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2-SHA256, 100k rounds (default for new data).
title: EncryptionFormat.modern
summary: AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2-SHA256, 100k rounds (default for new data).

rawIndexableTextContent: Creates a vault info with the given password and optional salt.  A non-empty password. An optional salt string. If nil or empty, the default salt is used. Return Value A VaultInfo, or nil if the password is empty.

For any values a and b, a != b implies that a == b is false. This is the default implementation of the not-equal-to operator (!=) for any type that conforms to Equatable.
summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.

rawIndexableTextContent: The encryption format to use when encrypting data.
title: EncryptionFormat
summary: The encryption format to use when encrypting data.

rawIndexableTextContent: RNCryptor v3: AES-256-CBC with PBKDF2-SHA1, 10k rounds (backward compatible).
title: EncryptionFormat.legacy
summary: RNCryptor v3: AES-256-CBC with PBKDF2-SHA1, 10k rounds (backward compatible).

rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system wrapper that encrypts files before uploading and decrypts them after downloading.  Overview Files are stored in a password-derived vault directory with a .cdecrypt extension. This is compatible with the Ensembles 2 ObjC CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystem. By default, new data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM (modern format). Set encryptionFormat to .legacy for RNCryptor v3 compatibility. Decryption auto-detects the format.
title: EncryptedCloudFileSystem
summary: A cloud file system wrapper that encrypts files before uploading and decrypts them after downloading.

rawIndexableTextContent: The salt used for vault path derivation (not the encryption salt).
title: salt
summary: The salt used for vault path derivation (not the encryption salt).
